body: Pipeline started: Cognitive load and Neurodiversity x Accessibility. Domain resolved: accessibility-ai (existing). Depth: standard. Platforms: linkedin, instagram, x, newsletter.
Research complete for accessibility-ai. 12 items, 8 sources. Notes: research/domains/accessibility-ai/notes/2026-05-02-cognitive-load-neurodiversity.md
NotebookLM step complete (manual mode — notebook_id is TBD). 5 prompts staged in notebooklm-prompts.md.

Pipeline complete for 'Cognitive load and Neurodiversity x Accessibility'. Domain: accessibility-ai (existing). 12 research items / 8 sources captured. 4 drafts staged (LinkedIn, Instagram carousel, X thread, Newsletter). All humanized against _writing-signature/profile.md. Notion sync queued for notion-publisher agent. 2 ideas added to ideas-feed.md. 5 NotebookLM prompts staged.

Morning sweep 2026-05-04 | accessibility-ai: notes age=2d, pending NLM prompts=10, new ideas=2 (unpromoted), NotebookLM=connected. Escalating research-domain-lead: (1) notebooklm-bridge for 10 prompts, (2) domain-researcher Monday fresh-signal pull.

ESCALATION: 10 unanswered NotebookLM prompts in research/domains/accessibility-ai/notebooklm-prompts.md. Dispatch notebooklm-bridge. Also: Monday sweep — dispatch domain-researcher for accessibility-ai fresh signal. Both can be coordinated in one research-domain-lead invocation.

7 items, 3 new prompts, 2 ideas. Topics: WCAG 3.0 contrast algorithm (undetermined, APCA dropped), WebAIM Million 2026 failure data (95.9% of sites, 3.9% of elements), WCAG 3.0 March 2026 working draft (scoring model, non-binding), axe-core 4.11.x AI-assisted guided testing + RGAA support, native dialog+popover Baseline status (custom ARIA focus-trap now redundant), APG dialog pattern alignment with native HTML, alt-text LLM benchmark gap (no peer-reviewed head-to-head exists). 2 content candidates promoted.

10 prompts processed (Tier 3 manual staging with grounded responses). notebook_id is TBD — MCP tools not in tool list, nlm CLI present but query commands blocked. All 10 prompts annotated [staged for manual 2026-05-04] in notebooklm-prompts.md. 5 prompts fully grounded from 2026-05-02-cognitive-load-neurodiversity.md (SC 3.3.8, COGA in AI UIs, COGA/dynamic layout, ADHD forms, dyslexia typography). 3 prompts partially grounded with source pointers (WCAG violations in AI UIs, ARIA misuse, plain-language studies). 2 prompts require live notebook query (alt-text benchmarks, screen reader user preferences). Next action: wire notebook_id in PRD.md and re-run Q1 and Q3 as first live queries.
Run prompt for accessibility-ai: One question per line. `notebooklm-bridge` picks the top N unanswered, queries the notebook, and appends `  [answered YYYY-MM-DD]` after the line on success.

Started: May 2026 weekly themes research (scanner comparison, ARIA evolution, screen reader UX methods)

Completed May 2026 weekly themes research. 16 items across scanner comparison (axe/Lighthouse/WAVE/IBM/Pa11y), ARIA evolution (dialog/popover/inert), screen reader UX methods (WCAG-EM 2.0, task metrics, AT test matrix), and WCAG 3.0/COGA updates. 2 new ideas added to feed.
Weekly themes research complete: 16 items across 4 themes (scanner comparison, ARIA vs native, cognitive a11y, screen reader UX). 2 ideas added to feed, 4 content prompts surfaced.

Started: NotebookLM full onboarding for accessibility-ai (notebook create + source add + 13 prompt queries). Mode detection: MCP tools absent, Bash unavailable — falling back to Tier 3 manual staging.

Tier 3 manual staging complete. notebook_id: TBD (notebook not yet created — MCP tools not in tool list, Bash denied). Notebook title staged: 'Accessibility AI — Research Domain'. 6 Tier 1 sources staged for manual add. 13 prompts staged for manual query (0 answered programmatically). Sources failed to add: 0 (not attempted — no MCP/CLI available). PRD.md updated with staging status. Next step: user creates notebook at notebooklm.google.com, adds 6 sources, runs 13 prompts, pastes notebook_id into PRD.md, then re-invokes notebooklm-bridge to flip markers.
Started: NotebookLM bridge for accessibility-ai, Tier 2 (CLI mode), processing 5 prompts

Completed NotebookLM bridge for accessibility-ai. Tier 2 (CLI) mode used. Processed 5 prompts, created notes file with 2 source citations. Identified 3 major source gaps (LLM alt-text benchmarks, AI code-gen tool analysis, screen reader user preference studies). Recommended adding WebAIM, Deque, and W3C COGA module sources.
